techniques.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.evidence_quote: the attacker directly accessed IMDS. IMDS v1 ... obtain the AccessKeyId, SecretAccessKey, and temporary token
through a worker's temporary credentials in Instance Metadata Service (IMDS) v1
clear-text IAM user access key and secret key in the terraform.tfstate file inside of an S3 bucket
find additional AWS credentials related to IAM users ... Lambda function's environment variables
they started gathering information about the resources deployed in the AWS account
The attacker was able to list the bucket available and retrieve all of the data
the harmless GetCallerIdentity API, which is permitted by default

techniques.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
techniques.evidence_quote: the attacker succeeded in disabling some of the logs configured in the account
trying to create new users, groups, and bind new access keys to existing IAM users
bind new access keys to existing IAM users

techniques.evidence_quote: The attacker used the credentials found in the previous step to move laterally. They directly contacted the AWS API
the attacker was able to retrieve and read more than 1 TB of information

attack_chain.description: Attacker exploited an internet-exposed service deployed in a self-managed Kubernetes cluster hosted in AWS, gaining access to a pod/container.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The attacker found and exploited an internet-exposed service deployed in a Kubernetes cluster.

evidence_chunks.quote: The attacker found and exploited an internet-exposed service deployed in a Kubernetes cluster.
The attacker found and exploited an internet-exposed service deployed in a Kubernetes cluster.

attack_chain.technique_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell
attack_chain.description: Attacker executed miner.sh shell script inside the compromised container to launch XMRig cryptominer.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker launched the script miner.sh in order to run an XMRig executable

evidence_chunks.quote: the attacker launched the script miner.sh in order to run an XMRig executable
the attacker launched the script miner.sh in order to run an XMRig executable

attack_chain.description: XMRig cryptominer executed on the compromised container to mine cryptocurrency (and/or act as a decoy).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: launch a cryptominer in order to make money or provide a distraction

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: Attacker queried IMDSv1 from the pod to retrieve the EC2 worker node IAM role's temporary credentials (AccessKeyId, SecretAccessKey, token).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker directly accessed IMDS ... obtain the AccessKeyId, SecretAccessKey, and temporary token

attack_chain.description: Attacker attempted to create new IAM users/groups and bind new access keys to existing IAM users for persistence; attempts were denied due to lack of permissions.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: trying to create new users, groups, and bind new access keys to existing IAM users

evidence_chunks.quote: trying to create new users, groups, and bind new access keys to existing IAM users
trying to create new users, groups, and bind new access keys to existing IAM users

attack_chain.description: Using the stolen cluster role credentials, attacker enumerated AWS resources including Lambda functions and S3 buckets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: they started gathering information about the resources deployed in the AWS account

evidence_chunks.quote: they started gathering information about the resources deployed in the AWS account
they started gathering information about the resources deployed in the AWS account

attack_chain.description: Attacker listed S3 buckets and enumerated their contents to identify valuable data and credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The attacker was able to list the bucket available and retrieve all of the data

evidence_chunks.quote: The attacker was able to list the bucket available and retrieve all of the data
The attacker was able to list the bucket available and retrieve all of the data

attack_chain.description: Attacker retrieved over 1 TB of data from S3 buckets including customer scripts, troubleshooting tools, logging files, and Terraform state files.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker was able to retrieve and read more than 1 TB of information

evidence_chunks.quote: the attacker was able to retrieve and read more than 1 TB of information
the attacker was able to retrieve and read more than 1 TB of information

attack_chain.description: Attacker downloaded Lambda function code using curl/wget, exfiltrating proprietary software and embedded keys.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Using curl or wget commands, the attacker successfully exfiltrated the Lambda code

evidence_chunks.quote: Using curl or wget commands, the attacker successfully exfiltrated the Lambda code
Using curl or wget commands, the attacker successfully exfiltrated the Lambda code

attack_chain.description: Attacker examined Lambda function environment variables and discovered additional IAM user credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: look at the Lambda function's environment variables and find additional AWS credentials related to IAM users

evidence_chunks.quote: look at the Lambda function's environment variables and find additional AWS credentials related to IAM users
look at the Lambda function's environment variables and find additional AWS credentials related to IAM users

attack_chain.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
attack_chain.description: Attacker invoked StopLogging on CloudTrail trails to disable logging and evade detection, succeeding with some trails.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker succeeded in disabling some of the logs configured in the account

evidence_chunks.quote: the attacker succeeded in disabling some of the logs configured in the account
the attacker succeeded in disabling some of the logs configured in the account

attack_chain.description: Using tools like Pacu and TruffleHog, attacker discovered clear-text IAM access key and secret key in terraform.tfstate files stored in S3, belonging to a second connected AWS account.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: clear-text IAM user access key and secret key in the terraform.tfstate file inside of an S3 bucket

attack_chain.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
attack_chain.description: Attacker used the newly discovered IAM credentials to authenticate into a second connected AWS account and attempt further enumeration, but nearly all API calls failed due to insufficient permissions.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker restarted their enumeration and information-gathering process ... all of the requests failed due to a lack of permissions, leaving just the harmless GetCallerIdentity API
